# n-dx

> Codebase analysis generated by Sourcevision.
> When using this document: group related items together and separate unrelated concerns.

## Project Overview

- **Files**: 1111
- **Lines of code**: 352,261
- **Languages**: TypeScript (932), JavaScript (45), CSS (44), Markdown (41), JSON (28)
- **Zones**: 21 (21 with descriptions)
- **Git**: feature/sv-fixes-0306 @ 8c2a2e8
- **Analyzed**: 2026-03-11T15:15:59.975Z

## Architecture Zones

### Chunked Review (`chunked-review`)

Satellite CLI zone providing the chunked-review command for staged PRD analysis, grouped with its batch-types contract and unit tests.

- Files: 5
- Cohesion: 0.36, Coupling: 0.64
- Entry points: packages/rex/src/analyze/batch-types.ts, packages/rex/src/cli/commands/chunked-review.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/chunked-review/context.md`

### Viewer Crash Recovery (`crash`)

Encapsulates crash detection, recovery state management, and recovery UI within the web viewer.

- Files: 8
- Cohesion: 0.60, Coupling: 0.40
- Entry points: packages/web/src/viewer/components/crash-recovery-banner.ts, packages/web/src/viewer/hooks/use-crash-recovery.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/crash/context.md`

### Monorepo E2E Tests (`e2e`)

The top-level end-to-end test suite validating CLI contracts, architecture policy, MCP transport, domain isolation, and orchestration behaviour across all packages.

- Files: 22
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/e2e/context.md`

### Hench Agent (`hench-agent`)

The autonomous execution engine that picks PRD tasks, builds briefs, runs tool-use loops via the Claude API, and records run history.

- Files: 141
- Cohesion: 0.99, Coupling: 0.01
- Entry points: packages/hench/src/prd/llm-gateway.ts, packages/hench/src/schema/v1.ts, packages/hench/src/tools/shell.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/hench-agent/context.md`

### Hench Guard (`hench-guard`)

Safety guardrail layer for the hench agent that enforces command allow-lists, path policies, and execution contracts before any shell or file operation runs.

- Files: 10
- Cohesion: 0.80, Coupling: 0.20
- Entry points: packages/hench/src/guard/index.ts, packages/hench/src/guard/paths.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/hench-guard/context.md`

### Landing Page (`landing-page`)

The standalone landing page service and its unit test, fully isolated from the main viewer and server zones.

- Files: 2
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/landing-page/context.md`

### LLM Foundation (`llm-foundation`)

Vendor-neutral LLM abstraction layer providing API and CLI providers, authentication, config, token parsing, and model resolution shared across hench and web.

- Files: 41
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/llm-foundation/context.md`

### Monorepo Integration Tests (`monorepo-integration-tests`)

Cross-package integration test suite verifying gateway contracts, scheduler startup, and server-viewer boundaries at the monorepo level.

- Files: 5
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/monorepo-integration-tests/context.md`

### Monorepo Orchestration (`monorepo-orchestration`)

Top-level entry points and configuration scripts that wire together all packages via spawn — the orchestration tier of the four-tier hierarchy.

- Files: 18
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/monorepo-orchestration/context.md`

### PRD Fix Command (`prd-fix-command`)

Satellite CLI zone encapsulating the fix command handler and its supporting core utility, paired with their unit tests.

- Files: 3
- Cohesion: 0.25, Coupling: 0.75
- Entry points: packages/rex/src/cli/commands/fix.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/prd-fix-command/context.md`

### Rex CLI E2E (`rex-cli-e2e`)

End-to-end test zone validating Rex CLI commands for the analyze, import, and prune workflows against a real filesystem.

- Files: 4
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/rex-cli-e2e/context.md`

### Rex Core Utilities (`rex-core-utilities`)

Isolated utility zone providing keyword extraction and criteria verification primitives consumed by the main Rex PRD Engine.

- Files: 4
- Cohesion: 0.18, Coupling: 0.82
- Entry points: packages/rex/src/core/keywords.ts, packages/rex/src/core/verify.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/rex-core-utilities/context.md`

### Rex E2E Config (`rex-e2e-config`)

Rex package-level e2e test suite configuration, test runner setup, and generated artifacts that sit outside the main source and unit-test zones.

- Files: 8
- Cohesion: 0.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/rex-e2e-config/context.md`

### Rex PRD Engine (`rex-prd-engine`)

Core domain layer for Rex containing the PRD store, schema, tree operations, analyze pipeline, and CLI output formatting.

- Files: 217
- Cohesion: 0.98, Coupling: 0.02
- Entry points: packages/rex/src/analyze/index.ts, packages/rex/src/analyze/validate-modification.ts, packages/rex/src/cli/commands/constants.ts, packages/rex/src/cli/commands/format-loe.ts, packages/rex/src/cli/output.ts (+3 more)
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/rex-prd-engine/context.md`

### Server Usage Scheduler (`server-usage-scheduler`)

Server-side task usage tracking, cleanup scheduling, and shared type definitions that support the web server's background maintenance and metrics pipeline.

- Files: 15
- Cohesion: 0.87, Coupling: 0.13
- Entry points: packages/web/src/server/shared-types.ts, packages/web/src/server/task-usage.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/server-usage-scheduler/context.md`

### Sourcevision Analysis Engine (`sourcevision-analysis-engine`)

The complete static analysis package implementing inventory, import graph, zone detection, component catalog, and findings pipelines for AI-readable codebase output.

- Files: 133
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Entry points: packages/web/src/server/domain-gateway.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/sourcevision-analysis-engine/context.md`

### Viewer PRD Interaction Layer (`viewer-prd-interaction`)

Groups the PRD view page, bulk-actions component, and lightweight UI hooks (feature-toggle, toast) that together power the interactive PRD editing surface.

- Files: 4
- Cohesion: 0.26, Coupling: 0.74
- Entry points: packages/web/src/viewer/views/prd.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/viewer-prd-interaction/context.md`

PRD tree interaction panels, analytics views, polling dispatchers, and build configuration files that form the dashboard-facing UI layer of the web package.

- Files: 33
- Cohesion: 0.53, Coupling: 0.47
- Entry points: packages/web/src/viewer/components/prd-tree/facet-filter.ts, packages/web/src/viewer/components/prd-tree/index.ts, packages/web/src/viewer/components/prd-tree/tree-search.ts, packages/web/src/viewer/hooks/use-facet-state.ts, packages/web/src/viewer/polling/batched-tick-dispatcher.ts (+7 more)
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/web-dashboard-platform/context.md`

### Web Integration Tests (`web-integration-tests`)

Contains the web package's integration-level boundary and build-output contract tests that enforce architectural governance rules automatically.

### Web Viewer Hub (`web-viewer`)

The 300-file composition hub of the web package containing the Preact viewer, server entry points, schema definitions, MCP handlers, and gateway modules that wire the full dashboard application.

- Files: 300
- Cohesion: 0.94, Coupling: 0.06
- Entry points: packages/web/src/server/rex-gateway.ts, packages/web/src/shared/view-id.ts, packages/web/src/viewer/components/logos.ts, packages/web/src/viewer/components/prd-tree/add-item-form.ts, packages/web/src/viewer/components/prd-tree/delete-confirmation.ts (+19 more)
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/web-viewer/context.md`

A pair of orphaned unit test files for viewer accessibility and status-filter behaviour that share no internal import edges.

- Files: 2
- Cohesion: 0.00, Coupling: 1.00
- Entry points: packages/web/src/viewer/components/guide.ts, packages/web/src/viewer/components/prd-tree/status-filter.ts

## Route Structure

### Server API Routes

**/api/hench/adaptive/** (handleAdaptiveRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-adaptive.ts`

```
DELETE  /api/hench/adaptive/override/:key
GET     /api/hench/adaptive/analysis
GET     /api/hench/adaptive/history
GET     /api/hench/adaptive/settings
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/apply
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/dismiss/:id
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/lock/:key
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/override
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/settings
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/unlock/:key
```

**/api/ndx-config** (handleConfigRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-config.ts`

```
GET     /api/ndx-config
GET     /api/projects
POST    /api/projects/switch
```

**/api/features** (handleFeaturesRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-features.ts`

```
GET     /api/features
PUT     /api/features
```

**/api/hench/** (handleHenchRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-hench.ts`

```
DELETE  /api/hench/templates/:id
GET     /api/hench/audit
GET     /api/hench/concurrency
GET     /api/hench/config
GET     /api/hench/execute/status
GET     /api/hench/execute/status/:taskId
GET     /api/hench/memory
GET     /api/hench/memory/history
GET     /api/hench/memory/history/:taskId
GET     /api/hench/memory/leaks
GET     /api/hench/metrics
GET     /api/hench/metrics/snapshots
GET     /api/hench/runs
GET     /api/hench/runs/:id
GET     /api/hench/runs/health
GET     /api/hench/task-usage
GET     /api/hench/templates
GET     /api/hench/templates/:id
GET     /api/hench/throttle
POST    /api/hench/execute
POST    /api/hench/execute/:taskId/terminate
POST    /api/hench/runs/:id/mark-stuck
POST    /api/hench/templates
POST    /api/hench/templates/:id/apply
POST    /api/hench/throttle/emergency-stop
POST    /api/hench/throttle/pause
POST    /api/hench/throttle/resume
PUT     /api/hench/config
PUT     /api/hench/throttle
```

**/api/** (handleIntegrationRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-integrations.ts`

```
DELETE  /api/integrations/:id/config
GET     /api/integrations
GET     /api/integrations/:id/config
GET     /api/integrations/:id/schema
PUT     /api/integrations/:id/config
```

**/api/notion/** (handleNotionRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-notion.ts`

```
DELETE  /api/notion/config
GET     /api/notion/config
POST    /api/notion/schema
POST    /api/notion/schema/fix
POST    /api/notion/test
PUT     /api/notion/config
```

**/api/project** (handleProjectRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-project.ts`

```
GET     /api/project
```

**/api/search** (handleSearchRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-search.ts`

```
GET     /api/search?q=<query>&limit=N
```

**/api/sv/** (handleSourcevisionRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-sourcevision.ts`

```
GET     /api/sv/components
GET     /api/sv/context
GET     /api/sv/imports
GET     /api/sv/inventory
GET     /api/sv/manifest

```

**/api/hench/workflow/** (handleWorkflowRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-workflow.ts`

```
GET     /api/hench/workflow/analysis
GET     /api/hench/workflow/history
POST    /api/hench/workflow/apply
POST    /api/hench/workflow/suggestions/:id
```

### Components: 5 total, 0 usage edges

## Findings

### Anti-patterns

- batch-types.ts is physically located in packages/rex/src/analyze/ (rex-prd-engine territory) but zone-classified under chunked-review — this misclassification makes the 2 reverse imports from rex-prd-engine appear as an upward dependency when they may be the engine importing its own types. Move batch-types.ts to packages/rex/src/cli/commands/ or reclassify it under rex-prd-engine to resolve the ambiguity. [warning] (chunked-review)
- crash/index.ts barrel imports crash-recovery-banner.ts from a parent/sibling directory (components/) rather than from within the crash/ subdirectory, inverting the normal barrel-enclosure pattern. This makes crash/ the only zone barrel in the web package that reaches upward — moving crash-recovery-banner.ts into the crash/ subdirectory would complete the consolidation already recommended by existing zone insights and restore the conventional barrel direction. [warning] (crash)
- 17 of 22 e2e test files have no documented priority status (required, sole-coverage, or disposable). Only the 5 tests named in CLAUDE.md have skip-prevention documentation. Without explicit classification, the undocumented tests are indistinguishable from expendable tests during triage, making them vulnerable to accidental exclusion in CI configuration changes. [warning] (e2e)
- hench-guard imports agent internals (4 cross-zone imports) to enforce policy, making guard correctness dependent on agent implementation details — if agent internals are refactored the guard silently loses enforcement capability; a stable injected policy interface would break this implementation-level dependency. [warning] (hench-guard)
- core/fix.ts has a single documented consumer (cli/commands/fix.ts) and lives outside the cli/ directory boundary, violating both the two-consumer rule and the satellite zone CLI-only content policy simultaneously. Inlining it into fix.ts severs the reverse edge into rex-prd-engine and closes both violations in one operation. [warning] (prd-fix-command)
- The fix and chunked-review commands are the highest-governance-risk CLI commands in the rex package (dual-fragility zones, active bidirectional cycles with the domain engine) but neither has an e2e test file. cli-fix.test.ts and cli-chunked-review.test.ts should be added before any structural consolidation to provide black-box regression coverage. [warning] (rex-cli-e2e)
- rex-core-utilities has zero internal imports between its two production files and exists solely because both are co-imported by rex-prd-engine — it is a community-detection artifact, not an architectural boundary. Dissolving it by moving keywords.ts and verify.ts into rex-prd-engine eliminates 11 cross-zone edges and three governance alerts with no behavior change. [warning] (rex-core-utilities)
- Domain engine (rex-prd-engine) imports from CLI satellite zone (chunked-review) at rex-unit → rex-cli: 2 — this is the most critical layering violation in the rex package because it means a CLI handler change can break domain engine behavior at load time in ESM/bundled contexts. [critical] (rex-prd-engine)
- The callback-injection decoupling pattern used by server-usage-scheduler (injecting broadcast, loadPRD, collectAllIds at startup) is not documented in CLAUDE.md or in gateway module documentation. Without documentation, future contributors may bypass the injection seam and introduce direct viewer imports to restore 'simpler' access, undoing the decoupling. The pattern should be captured in the gateway module table as a named exemption: 'scheduler callbacks — injected at startup, not imported.' [info] (server-usage-scheduler)
- viewer-prd-interaction contains structurally incompatible file archetypes: a page-level route component (prd.ts, singleton by definition) and reusable utilities (use-toast, use-feature-toggle, bulk-actions.ts). Pages are composition roots that can never satisfy the two-consumer rule; utilities must satisfy it. Mixing both archetypes in a dual-fragility zone makes zone-level governance unenforceable — the correct fix is full zone dissolution into web-viewer rather than partial hook extraction that leaves a 2-file residual zone still below the reliable-metric threshold. [warning] (viewer-prd-interaction)
- ... and 5 more

### Patterns

- batch-types.ts is physically in packages/rex/src/analyze/ but zone-classified under chunked-review — moving the file to packages/rex/src/cli/commands/ would align physical location with zone ownership and eliminate the masked boundary crossing. [warning] (chunked-review)
- crash-detector.ts intentionally bypasses external.ts to import ViewId directly from shared/ — this is a documented cycle-prevention exemption validated by boundary-check.test.ts. Other zones needing to avoid bidirectional cycles should follow this same direct-shared-import pattern rather than routing through the viewer gateway. [info] (crash)
- cli-dev.test.js is listed in CLAUDE.md as a required test and is the sole gate for dev-mode startup coverage. If this file is skipped, flaked, or times out in CI, dev-mode regressions become undetectable. It should have an explicit timeout budget and a CI status check that alerts on skip (not just failure) to prevent silent coverage gaps. [warning] (e2e)
- hench-agent (141 files, coupling 0.01) is the only large zone (100+ files) in the monorepo with near-zero outbound coupling — its gateway-mediated isolation is the strongest evidence that the gateway pattern scales to large zones and should serve as the reference model for web-viewer boundary governance. [info] (hench-agent)
- landing-page has zero inbound and zero outbound cross-zone imports — it is the only zone in the web package with no coupling surface, making it safe to refactor, delete, or rebuild in isolation [info] (landing-page)
- The foundation tier boundary (llm-foundation) is the best-enforced tier boundary in the monorepo: only two gateways (hench/src/prd/llm-gateway.ts and web/src/server/domain-gateway.ts) cross it, and no leaf file in any other zone imports directly from llm-client — this is the only tier where the gateway contract has zero known violations. [info] (llm-foundation)
- Zero coupling from test zone to production zones is a strong positive signal: all five tests exercise the system through spawned processes, making the test zone immune to the circular-dependency risks present in production zones. [info] (monorepo-integration-tests)
- Orchestration tier achieves (cohesion 1.0, coupling 0.0) via spawn-only; hench-agent achieves (0.99, 0.01) via gateway pattern; web zones average (~0.7, ~0.4) with convention-only enforcement — the health gradient directly tracks enforcement mechanism strength: spawn > gateway > convention. [info] (monorepo-orchestration)
- The fix satellite zone is the smallest zone (4 files) producing a return edge into the domain engine — the structural remedy is simpler here than in chunked-review: inlining core/fix.ts into fix.ts would close the cycle and collapse the zone to 3 files without losing any behavior. [warning] (prd-fix-command)
- chunked-review and prd-fix-command are the two highest-risk CLI commands (dual-fragility governance, active bidirectional cycles with the domain engine) but neither has a corresponding e2e test file in rex-cli-e2e — adding e2e coverage for these commands would surface cycle-induced initialization failures that unit tests cannot detect. [warning] (rex-cli-e2e)
- ... and 10 more

### Relationships

- rex-prd-engine imports from chunked-review (2 crossings), meaning the CLI satellite zone is not a pure leaf node — it introduces a load-order dependency from the domain layer onto a CLI command handler, which can cause initialization failures in bundled or ESM contexts. [critical] (chunked-review)
- The crash→web-viewer bidirectional edge is driven entirely by the hook/component layer (use-crash-recovery.ts, crash-recovery-banner.ts), not by crash-detector.ts. Isolating the hook-layer coupling from the core detection logic preserves independent testability of the detection logic and prevents the bidirectional edge from growing further. [info] (crash)
- hench-guard ↔ hench-agent is the only bidirectional cycle outside the web package — it is also the only cycle where the smaller zone (10 files) imports into the larger (141 files) rather than the reverse, confirming this is a policy-layer inversion rather than organic coupling growth. [warning] (hench-guard)
- Integration test zone has no test targeting the rex-core ↔ rex-unit bidirectional import cycle — the only structural cycle flagged as critical in the codebase has no dedicated regression test at the integration layer. [warning] (monorepo-integration-tests)
- web-server-viewer-boundary test should assert an exhaustive allowlist of permitted web-server→web-viewer imports (currently 3), not just the absence of a known bad import — without an allowlist, new reverse imports silently pass. [warning] (monorepo-integration-tests)
- core/fix.ts sits outside cli/ but inside the prd-fix-command zone boundary and likely supplies the 1 return import that rex-prd-engine emits back — if core/fix.ts has only one consumer (fix.ts), it should be absorbed into the command handler, which would also sever the return edge and eliminate the cycle. [warning] (prd-fix-command)
- rex-cli-e2e imports from none of the satellite zones (chunked-review, prd-fix-command, rex-core-utilities) and produces zero cross-zone edges — this confirms the e2e suite tests the CLI as a black box rather than white-box testing internals, which is the correct pattern but also means internal zone cycles are invisible to e2e assertions. [info] (rex-cli-e2e)
- The 3 outbound imports from rex-core-utilities into rex-prd-engine (likely schema/type imports) combined with 8 inbound imports from the engine create a type-dependency loop — verify.ts or keywords.ts depend on types defined in the very zone that consumes them, which is structurally identical to a circular import even if the build currently resolves it. [critical] (rex-core-utilities)
- batch-types.ts is physically located in packages/rex/src/analyze/ (within the rex-prd-engine directory tree) but is classified under the chunked-review zone — this file-location/zone-membership mismatch is a leaky abstraction: callers of the analyze/ directory implicitly cross a zone boundary without any import path signal. [warning] (rex-prd-engine)
- rex-prd-engine imports back into chunked-review (rex-unit → rex-cli: 2 imports) — the main domain layer has an upward dependency on a CLI satellite zone, inverting the expected CLI-over-domain layering and creating a critical architecture violation. [critical] (rex-prd-engine)
- ... and 5 more

### Observations

- High coupling (0.64) — 7 imports target "rex-prd-engine" [warning] (chunked-review)
- Low cohesion (0.36) — files are loosely related, consider splitting this zone [warning] (chunked-review)
- Cohesion 0.36 and coupling 0.64 confirm dual-fragility status — additions must satisfy the two-consumer rule and CLI-only content policy per CLAUDE.md satellite zone governance. [warning] (chunked-review)
- With only 5 files the zone metrics are below the reliable-measurement threshold (5-file minimum); cohesion/coupling values reflect low internal relationship between a types file and two command handlers rather than structural decay. [info] (chunked-review)
- batch-types.ts is an entry point shared with rex-prd-engine; any change to batch type shapes will ripple into both the satellite zone and the main domain layer — treat it as a versioned contract. [info] (chunked-review)
- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (e2e)
- As the monorepo grows, consider grouping e2e tests into sub-directories by concern (cli/, architecture/, mcp/) to improve discoverability and allow targeted test runs. [info] (e2e)
- Cohesion 1.0 and zero coupling are the expected ideal for an e2e test zone — the suite is architecturally clean. [info] (e2e)
- cli-dev.test.js and the scheduler-startup integration test are the sole coverage gates for dev-mode startup and scheduler boot respectively; protect them from accidental skip or timeout in CI pipelines. [info] (e2e)
- Bidirectional coupling: "server-usage-scheduler" ↔ "web-viewer" (3+39 crossings) — consider extracting shared interface [warning]
- ... and 81 more

### Suggestions

- Zone "crash" has files across 5 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory [info] (crash)
- Zone "crash" was identified from filename patterns, not directory structure — consider creating packages/web/src/viewer/components/crash/ and moving related files there [info] (crash)
- Move packages/web/tests/helpers/crash-detector-test-support.ts into packages/web/tests/unit/viewer/ alongside crash-detector.test.ts to eliminate the singleton helpers/ directory. The file has exactly one consumer (crash-detector.test.ts), so there is no sharing benefit from a separate directory. [info] (crash)
- packages/rex/tests/e2e/ contains files from 3 zones (rex-cli-e2e, rex-e2e-config, rex-prd-engine) — consider grouping into subdirectories to clarify architectural boundaries [info]
- packages/web/tests/unit/server/ contains files from 4 zones (rex-prd-engine, server-usage-scheduler, sourcevision-analysis-engine, web-viewer) — consider grouping into subdirectories to clarify architectural boundaries [info]
- Add a build-file exclusion list to sourcevision configuration: packages/web/build.js, packages/web/dev.js, and any future *.config.js or build-tooling files should be excluded from zone membership analysis. These files share directory proximity with runtime components but have no runtime import relationships with them — their inclusion corrupts cohesion and coupling metrics for any zone they land in. [warning]
- Add a cross-reference in hench-rex-gateway-pipeline.test.js JSDoc to '@see TESTING.md — gateway admission criterion', matching the pattern established in gateway-pipeline.test.js. Consistent cross-references across all gateway pipeline tests ensure engineers find the formal admission rules regardless of which file they encounter first. [info]
- Add a hooks/index.ts barrel to packages/web/src/viewer/hooks/ and a boundary-check.test.ts assertion requiring consumers to import through the barrel. The hooks directory is the largest leaf-import surface in the web viewer (29 files) with no enforcement — analogous to the pre-barrel state of crash/ and shared/ before their boundary assertions were added. [warning]
- Augment the integration test growth policy in TESTING.md and integration-coverage-policy.test.js to enforce a minimum test-case-per-boundary count (not just file count). The current file-count ratio can be gamed by adding empty skeleton files; a per-seam minimum (e.g., at least 2 test cases per documented architectural boundary) would enforce substantive coverage growth. [info]
- Both rex-core-utilities (0 internal imports between 2 production files) and rex-e2e-config (0 internal imports between 8 files) have zero production cohesion. Add an explicit rule to zone governance: any zone where no two production files share an import edge is a detection artifact, not an architectural zone. Documenting this classification criterion would allow automated filtering of artifact zones from governance dashboards. [info]
- ... and 61 more

## Tooling

This project uses n-dx for codebase analysis (Sourcevision) and requirement
tracking (Rex). MCP tools are available for interactive access to analysis
data and PRD management. Skills available: /plan, /status, /capture,
/zone, /work, /configure.
